intro: In his 9 years on Audiotool, Naswalt has developed from a quirky underdog to one of the most important and influential musicians in the community. His hybrid tracks, consisting of recordings from his own home studio and the Audiotool studio, are as indispensable for the charts as his entertaining videos for social media.
about: I grew up in bum country Tennessee with an internet connection that led me to audiotool almost 10 years ago when I was a freshman in high school. I grew up with audiotool.

tracks.desc: Nominal was one of the first artists I truly felt like was pushing the envelope on what audiotool could make.
I saw this track on audiotool when I had just started listening to LCD Soundsystem.
Xavi has always been an amazing artist who I've looked up to, but this song gave me the synthwave bug.
Sim is great at distortion. THIS song is what anger sounds like.
Goon deserves it. This track was the first where I had to keep listening to it over and over again.
My own track - the highlight of the whole experience for me.

intro: Goon has also been an active part of our valued community for many years. Three attributes always apply: coolness, funk and danceability.
about: I've been hanging out on Audiotool for about 9 years, and am currently based in the middle of a field in the south of England.

tracks.desc: opaqity - one of my favorite tracks
Floating Points inspired

intro: With his crystal clear voice, his great compositions and his production skills, no one would be surprised if Icebox became a big player in the international Pop Music scene.
about: I'm a 17 year old student/producer taking a Diploma for Music and Audio Technology in Singapore Polytechnic.

tracks.desc: confused by naswalt & sim - first to encourage me to sing
Come Alive by Pixus Decay - overlooked genius
Scrolls by SIREN X OFV - fusion of multiple genres
cirrus - Tim Derry Remix - beautiful acoustic journey
Lost to Time by Velocistar - my biggest mentor
Valentine by Icebox & sim - my best work, hands down

intro: With his mix of chilled beats, a dose of thoughtfulness and a fair amount of Hip Hop, Fluent hit the mark and quickly played his way into the hearts of the community.
about: I'm from Phoenix, Arizona. I have been using Audiotool for about 5 years now.

tracks.desc: opaqity - one of my favorite tracks, conveys so much emotion
tornsage - the intro gets me every time
pen. - so powerful, still impressed after 4 years
ford. - the last chord is so emotional, nobody does it like Ford
letter to the sun by nico - super unique, vocals fit perfectly
That Sinking Feeling - my own favorite track

intro: Jordan Kibbey plays the claviatur of airy bass music like one of the greats. His compositions always seem mature and original.
about: I am from Cleveland Ohio and I have been on the site for around 7 years.

tracks.desc: opaqity always made 'complete' works.
Jetdarc is my favorite chiptune producer ever.
arrow arrow - a melody can last an entire song if it's good.
feel something - being ok with getting weird.
maroon gate - endgame chord progressions.
Schizophrenic Turtles - my own track.

intro: Syntax has been enriching the Audiotool output for a very long time with his versatile but always unerring and mostly danceable genre mix.
about: Audiotool artist of over 10 years from Winnipeg, Manitoba and just turning 27 years old.

tracks.desc: Astrum - my biggest inspiration, still a masterpiece
Infyuthsion - one of my favourite DnB tracks in general

intro: Laevent's sound is always to the point, fresh, experimental and perfectly produced. Definitely one of the greats.
about: I am from southern Louisiana. I've been on audiotool for about 6-7 years now. I'm non-binary.

tracks.desc: My favorite track on Audiotool. It makes me super emotional every time.
OLSWEL! by viista - super hard.
QI - clear example that you can make hits on Audiotool.
trauma by etterath - visionary talent.
I EAT MERCURY by 1trillionMPH - powerful track.
ARTIFICIAL 2 - my own track, abrasive and emotive.

intro: High up in the north there lives a man who always manages to enchant Audiotool users with his Ambient sounds and acoustic recordings.
about: My name is Truls, from the far-off land of Norway. I see myself as just another hobbyist producer.

tracks.desc: zignabah - sometimes less is more
André Michelle - the Heisenberg showcase
Kepz - that melody has been living rent free in my head
tornsage - inspired me to use physical instruments
1991 by Wightfall - phenomenal sound design

intro: If you're looking for clean funky House or chilled EDM tunes on Audiotool, you can't overlook Tim Derry.
about: I'm a 21-year-old frenchie from Lyon, currently studying at HEC Paris.

tracks.desc: Odesi by Arcade - one of my favorite artists, groove master
On The Roads by Frigolito - probably the most talented musician here
juxtaposition by okin & naut - definitely one of the best things created recently
Teardrops ft. Nionsomnia - sounds really professional
pipe dream by retro - fresh air, true creativity
LEMON SODA FUNK - my own track, very fun to make

intro: Sila already made a name for himself with wacky HQ Lofi House tracks. Whatever he delivers, it is always massive.
about: I was born in Russia later on as a kid moved to Europe. I've been with Audiotool since late 2009.

tracks.desc: VLTRA - flower loop, on my all-time TOP list for 3 years
Tetrilax by Jetdarc - changed the way I see what can be done on AT
]push (how do) by nico - great example of NOISE being gorgeous
peace 4 luv by voyd - the right representation of LO-FI house
sonar converter by WPX & formaldehyde - masterpiece
datastructures - my own track, the biggest most complex IDM track on AT

intro: Vulkron can without exaggeration be described as a constant at the top of the Audiotool singles charts.
about: I'm from the humble country of America, specifically Florida. I've been on Audiotool for about 7 years.

tracks.desc: synthonix - never stopped loving the unique melodies
dove - I had no clue I would enjoy the chill vibes
Tocka - influenced my sound design a lot

intro: Bubblz's way of merging cool trap beats with heart-warming synth melodies and atmospheres is rare even outside Audiotool.
about: 3 years ago I was introduced to the creative freedom which is Audiotool. AND IT IS FREE?! WHAAT?!

tracks.desc: Places by Soph - embodiment of untouched music
become ghosts by X-O - bittersweet feeling
Frigolito - absolutely broke my mind
6AM by okin - beauty is sitting down and making what you feel
Tetrilax by Jetdarc - fell in love 10 seconds in

intro: Frogs in suits, audio memes, funny pictures - that's Joe. But he surprises in between with a well-considered dose of seriousness.
about: My name is Joe, I am 23 years old. I play ukulele, bass guitar, and drums.

tracks.desc: Amp7070 - I am blown away every time
aeiter - this track has made me cry on multiple occasions

intro: The last and one of the youngest artists in the first round is a guarantor for nasty basses and heavy drums.
about: My name is Vincent and I am 16 years old. I've been on here for like 2 years.

tracks.desc: joVee - ROADKILL_REFLEXES, one of the best Death Step artists
Project Epsilon - Crab Riddim
TwoSworded - Havok
Astrel - Found Me remix
Moosey - best trap producer on Audiotool
Beyond - my own track

intro: rai. has made a name for himself in recent years. His music is distinguished by a high degree of creativity and technical accuracy.
about: My name is Rasa Ichimori. I'm from Japan, but I'm now in Utah studying Business Management at BYU.

tracks.desc: Things To Come by 4▲4 - the 2nd song I listened to, convinced me to start
I Can't Explain by Ante - cold winter mornings on trains in Japan
there's always tomorrow by opaqity - was my alarm sound for a long time
Lonesome by Someone - made with a single 303, no boundaries
6282 by SOLACE - gave me an idea about chords
Ashamed by THRONE - one of the absolute legends of AT

intro: ▽Momo▽桃. is undoubtedly one of Audiotool's top-notch users. Her self-confident manner makes her a true star.
about: My name is Monique, I am from Africa / Nigeria and I've been with Audiotool for a rough estimate of 4 years.

tracks.desc: Cradle by Fluctus x Lvoe - got me into melodic trap and future bass
ＷΛＴΣＲＰΛＲＫ by 2k* - got me into heavy trap music
mystic by O R I O N - got me into melodic dark trap
lowrider by seb - got me into bouncy trap
Sad Boi Skio by TteeraBeats - got me into trap with percs
Iris. - my own track, encompasses my creativity

intro: Wightfall's music is not light fare, yet it's a pleasure to listen to his mix of indie/alternative and electro. His album Nine Steps is an Audiotool milestone.
about: I'm from GA, USA. I currently attend UGA/Oxford through a transfer program, studying philosophy and religion. I started on Audiotool when I was 12 after googling 'make music for free.'

tracks.desc: Four Hundred by opaqity - absolutely foundational music
A Dragon Named Cuddlex by Cuddlex - AT sound-design par excellence
detroit by looks - huge inspiration melodically, tonally, rhythmically
VVV by Xavi - I ripped off so much from this when I was younger
you were plastic by amoeba - so much emotion, beautiful sound design
Myrrh - (II) - my own track, first where I felt I adequately represented my style

intro: In more or less a decade on Audiotool, TS has not only grown up but solidified his musical style and now provides a foundation for the Bass Music community.
about: I've been on this site for about 8, going on 9 years now. I joined the site when I was in middle school.

tracks.desc: My own track Havok

intro: While his first steps on Audiotool five years ago were somewhat clumsy, today we can speak of vocal and music tracks indistinguishable from professional recordings.
about: My names Ethan, I'm 20 and based out of Richmond VA.

tracks.desc: Animal Prints by Xavi - the bounce is so intoxicating
lazy sad by looks & ford - studied on repeat trying to learn sidechain
terrae by naut - breathtaking ambient guitar ballet
Wanderlust by Tocka - immaculate vibes, showed to all my friends
would have been by nico - grown on me more with every listen
Did I Make You Proud? - my own track, wrote it for my dad

intro: To pigeonhole sim in one style would be a mistake. The lowest common denominator is the incredible quality that runs through all his productions.
about: I'm from the rural deep south, the state of Mississippi. It was around 8 or so years ago when I stumbled across Audiotool.

tracks.desc: Sleep Method II by VLTRA - sparked my love for wonky, spaced out beats
metaphorical by sky bound zoo - like a kid laying on a grassy hill on a perfect spring day
letter to the sun by nico - hugs and comforts you before taking you to another world
Electron by Infyuthsion - the first song I heard from this site
Ergo, The Night by Xavi - opened my ears to what synths are capable of
maybe i can actually do this for once - my own track, a good time capsule

intro: Aeiter is somehow the singer-songwriter of Audiotool. His vocals and instrumentation transport the listener into misty landscapes.
about: Hi, I'm Ethan. I like to take care of plants, bake bread and cook obscure foods. I discovered Audiotool when I was 11.

tracks.desc: FLYING by sky bound zoo - SBZ's musical ability confounds me
circles [remix] by sim - influenced how I approach deep lofi texture
small spaces by opaqity - first song I learned how to play on piano
Rise by Kepz - aided me in laying out the progression for my earlier tracks
MUGEN II by HBVI - the synth served as base for presets I still use today
habit - my own track, proud of the comb filtered basses

intro: okin's uplifting, dreamy mood runs like a thread through his work. His melodies skillfully span the arc between daydream and danceability.
about: 22, born and raised in the UK. I come from a very small town.

tracks.desc: Identical [ollie flip] by ollie - one of my favorites
stressed out (neo flip) by neo. - lush flip from aeiter's beautiful track
Pixel Puddle by Impavid - underrated is an understatement
Stray by Snio & illika - that deep bass just is so hmmm
Continuity by naut - amazing attention to detail
oberon - my own track, nice tense atmosphere

intro: Client is undoubtedly one of the heavy hitters in the Audiotool Bass Music scene. He delivers quality that leaves nothing to be desired.
about: Hi all! My real name is Cory. I'm originally from Sierra Vista, Arizona. But now I live near Dallas, in Texas. I am 19 years old.

tracks.desc: Irisu by Silica (Kurea) - my #1 fav track ever, truly tells a story
Kurea - Oni by Silica - first well designed modern dubstep track
MONIKER by @sndcult - one of the most well mixed tracks on Audiotool
Black light by Melancolist - huge inspiration for my Techno attempts
The Old World OST by Kyba & Zerod - reason why I make Sci-Fi genre
Odyssey - my own track, longest and most elaborate

intro: Looking back ten years, when Gravidon entered the Audiotool stage, it was clear from the start that we were not dealing with a beginner.
about: I was born and raised on the East Coast of the US, but I've recently moved to Texas. I was introduced to Audiotool a little over 10 years ago.

tracks.desc: worn by Audial - some of the best pads I've ever heard
You & I by TEQTONIQ - I still have trouble comprehending that Teq is real
outcry by amoeba - simple premise with larger than life ambience
THE FATHER by leadenshrew - some British dude that hates Elden Ring
feel something by rai. & five - can't convince me rai isn't a KOAN Sound burner
Chroma Force - my own track, like touching a Power Star in a Bugatti

intro: nico's musical background is fully apparent. There isn't a single track where you don't feel someone that breathes music is at work.
about: I just turned 21 last month and I'm currently in a dual enrollment program studying philosophy at Tufts and jazz composition at the New England Conservatory.

tracks.desc: idle idle by aeiter - this song has gotten me through so much
why did i fade away by opaqity - a masterpiece in sadness
Lonesome by Someone/Frigolito - no boundaries in Audiotool
nite owl by SOLACE - incredible vibe
maybe i can actually by sim - makes me feel like a warm Sunday afternoon
letter to the sun - my own track, what I wish all my tracks could be

intro: leadenshrew's tracks, always produced to the point, have all captivating instrumentation through a clear crispness that is second to none.
about: Hi, I'm Gabe. I'm 21, I live in the UK and I just finished my first year at university studying Television Production.

tracks.desc: egodeath w/ opi by looks - the best trap track on the website
why did i fade away by opaqity - most evocative and heart-breaking track
peripherals by abstract & looks - most infectious melodies I've ever heard
Fractals by Infyuthsion - person I'm most grateful for in my growth
im not gonna keep asking by evilpanda107 - my favourite song on Audiotool

intro: viista is undoubtedly one of Audiotool's wild ones. His mixture of noisy Trap and anarchic Lo-Fi Breakz often scratches the border of the bearable.
about: Hey yall, I'm from North Carolina but moved to Massachusetts for 10 years and then moved back to NC 4 or so years ago.

tracks.desc: nocturn by aeiter - inspired my downtempo style, cozy cabin vibe
thinking music by nico - helped when I was feeling anxious
Aura by ApoC - feels like an escape, cures cancer and depression
FireBreather by laevent - atomic bomb converted into audio format
FloridaWater! by magi - sitting outside clearing your mind
delicate - my own collab with sky bound zoo, my favorite release

intro: Melancolist's synth lines, inspired by the pioneers of electro music, are heartbreakingly beautiful. Your music is a blessing.
about: Hey! My name is François. I have the most French name ever. I live in the suburbs of Paris.

tracks.desc: looks - renegade, one of my all-time favorites
Frigolito - incredible production

intro: ApoC is a clear example of how, besides talent, discipline and hard work are also part of growing up as an artist.
about: I'm from the U.S., I've been using Audiotool since 2016. I love watching WWE.

tracks.desc: Forlorn by Waveshaper and Arcade - gets me emotional

intro: sphere's music remains only superficially inaccessible: once it has caught you, it unfolds a claustrophobic beauty that won't let go.
about: Hello, I'm Sphere. I'm 17, I'm from Texas, and I joined Audiotool around 7 years ago.

tracks.desc: Lonesome by Someone - one of the most ingenious works in any DAW
Call of the Void by opaqity - near-perfect show of the beauty of simplicity
Sore Loser by nico & solar plexus - absolute fucking beast of a track
Loose End by Wightfall - beautifully haunting melodies, near unparalleled
Inclusive Data by sila - expert-level sampling unlike anything seen before
It Ought To Be - my own track, the direction I've wanted for a long time

intro: Drimeur is one of the few remaining representatives of the old-school Tech-House genre. He sticks unerringly to the sound design of his idols.
about: Hi everyone this is Rafael speaking. I'm a 30-year-old French guy who discovered Audiotool 12 years ago.

tracks.desc: Shreddin' by Giles - great grooves, stunning basslines, total creativity
Q8 by Kepz - has such an underground touch that I love
MIDNIGHT WONDERS by Tim Derry - smiling, crying and headbanging at the same time
pack and get dressed by Snad Breugen - my spiritual and musical master
Déjà vu - my own track

intro: Andy is, without exaggeration, one of the great vocalists of Audiotool. He is not afraid to use his Pop Music-oriented timbre for experimental sound structures.
about: Howdy I'm Andy, 23, and have been on AT since 2012. I'm from Eastern Pennsylvania.

tracks.desc: socio.pass by fidelity - blew my mind first time and still does
wasteland by naswalt - one of his first hits, the vox and sax are top tier
Ghosts by Ace_Ex - I thought it wasn't made on Audiotool
bored by dove - Dove is the king of Audiotool in my opinion
pete by looks - simplest tracks but carried so much emotion
Pyrite - my own track, my best at the time

intro: Whether you're a fan of dark and introspective music or only appreciate great art, etterath is worth checking out.
about: Hiya! I'm Cherry and I'll be 17 years old by the time this interview is out! I was born in Nashville, but I've lived in central Florida for the past 8 years.

tracks.desc: Soon I'll Be (roses) by evilpanda107/withdecay - beautiful, haunting, vocals So Good
atlas by nico - such a creative and powerful piece
nostalgia by abstract - first artist that truly inspired me on AT
apogee by opaqity - may be one of the best artists period
stardust by looks - masterfully produced, powerful, stunning
trauma (we made it) - my own track, my most raw expression of emotion

intro: Just in time for Christmas, we're bringing Xavi back, who left Audiotool a few years ago to try his luck in the music business.
about: I have had the pleasure of touring with Seven Lions, playing multiple shows and more than a couple of festivals.

tracks.desc: Spaces by acloudyskye - where I realized how far they could go
apogee by opaqity - one of the most impressive tracks I've heard on AT
stardust by looks - literally perfect in just about every way
Moonrise by Kepz - knows how to use ambiance to set a stage
When Stars Collide by rnzr - one of the reasons I wanted to get good
AFTERGLOW by Astrum - perfect notes of nostalgia
I. (Mango Beach) - my own track, the best I ever did on here

intro: Voyd, alongside VLTRA and Sila, is undoubtedly a standout member of the Audiotool Lo-Fi community.
about: My name is Emre Girginkaya. I am 28 years old and I am from İstanbul/Turkey.

tracks.desc: VLTRA - flower loop, always on my all-time TOP list

intro: JayFK has been killing it since dropping his first track just a year ago. Whether Deep House or Techno, Jay's tracks always bring an authentic vibe.
about: Hello Audiotool community! I'm Jens and I live in a small town near Hannover in Germany. I am now 48 years old.

tracks.desc: My own track Vertigo

intro: Siren's Bass-Music-inspired Hip Hop and Drum and Bass have always exhibited a high degree of professional coolness.
about: I'm a 19 year old producer/rapper/DJ from Penang, Malaysia.

tracks.desc: sensory deprivation by sila and WPX - great balance

intro: Kepz's story is a pleasure to tell: as a young teenager, he played a significant role in shaping Audiotool's early years. Now he works at Audiotool.
about: I'm a Ukrainian born, Australian based artist, DJ and software engineer. I release and perform under my alias Kooscha.

tracks.desc: Q8 - my own track that Drimeur also picked
kitsch Kepz remix - my own remix
Rise - my own track

intro: WPX masterfully merges seemingly opposing styles to create a sound that is both unexpected and unconventional.
about: I was born in Detroit Michigan and have spent most of my life in New York. I've used Audiotool since I was around 9 or 10.

tracks.desc: My own collaboration with sila

intro: mxcii is the 150th featured artist of the second generation. With his crystal-clearly produced DnB pieces, he's at the forefront of his genre.
about: Hey there, my name is Luis Galán, aka mxcii. I'm 21 years old and I live in Copenhagen, Denmark.

tracks.desc: Audiotool Day 2014 by opaqity - massive inspiration, biggest impact on me
Escalator Tourists by abstract - what got me into making drum and bass
Beyond Dreams by looks - revelation that electronic music uses chords too

No Future by Aron & CGMan - shows how capable AT can be
SNOW (mxcii remix) - my own track, quite satisfied with it

intro: Retro is one of our inconspicuous heroes. His tracks bear witness to immense musicality and advanced know-how.
about: Hey, my name is Jonas. I'm from Cologne, Germany and I've been on Audiotool for 8 years.

tracks.desc: Astrum - secta, still a masterpiece

intro: Amp7070 describes himself as genre phobic, and his playful mix of melancholic beauty and whimsical humor is truly difficult to classify.
about: I am from Surrey in the UK, and am currently in my second year of studying psychology at the University of Sheffield.

tracks.desc: My own track - Hurdle over the hurdle

intro: With Civility we welcome another great singer-songwriter. His across-the-board sensitive, magnificently composed indie songs go straight to the ear and heart.
about: I'm originally from New York but currently reside in Florida for school. I've been with audiotool for Six years.

tracks.desc: figment by nico - everything about this song is perfected
Held by Fluent/owtlet - really enjoy what he did here
reflexive matters by anodyne - such an underrated artist
sim - has me jamming out every time
Body, Soul by Wightfall - feelings are almost overwhelming
Over My Head - my own, represents a new era for me

intro: Kurp undeniably stands as an artist who has discovered his distinctive style. His music blends industrial Techno rhythms, crying pads, and hypnotic arpeggios.
about: Greetings from Lithuania. I have been on audiotool since 2015 or 8 years. Currently I am 20.

tracks.desc: intricacies by kuatari

intro: Uzeh's consistent production output and dedication to refining her craft have made her an indispensable part of our trap community.
about: I am originally from the United States, however I currently live in Germany where I attend University.

tracks.desc: "$werve" by Vinnie et al. - foundation for a lot of my earlier work
sanctuary. by noah/itxmi - so proud of his growth
GLO Boy by KC - found me when I was small, taught me a lot
Shoot'n by BYE BROS - inspired me to make my own melodies
Going Insane by TteeraBeats - I find myself coming back every once in a while
i miss him. - my own track, I genuinely think it's perfect

intro: althruist embodies the spirit of altruism — approachable, generous, and deeply connected with our community. His music is not only alternative but also strikingly unconventional.
about: I'm from a little fish-shaped island in the middle of the Mediterranean sea (Malta), 17 years old.

tracks.desc: Runaway by acloudyskye - first song I remixed and dissected
dinosaur friends by embr & solar plexus - one of my first favourited songs
Repeater by acloudyskye - sound design that makes me listen on repeat 105 times
polyfiction by xoelmusic - sentimental, discovered when I joined
QUERENCIA by Tim Derry - something so summery
Voyage - my own track, describes my style best

intro: Le Voile started her musical journey 12 years ago on Audiotool as a teenager. Over time, she's grown into a confident, dynamic artist creating bass-heavy EDM tracks.
about: I'm LeVoile, an artist and producer with over 10 years of experience creating music on Audiotool.

tracks.desc: Explosion! VIP by RiMi & XculE - felt more excited than I had in a long time
TRAUMA by JayFK - shared my love for Rezz with him
Zero Light by Grawlix - convinced me there's real dubstep potential in AT
Voyage by althruist - beautiful and fun
Midnight Matters - my own track

intro: anodyne began his Audiotool journey crafting incredible atmospheric Synthwave. Now focusing on indie singer-songwriter, blending introspective lyrics with rich melodies.
about: I'm from New York, always have been. I've been with Audiotool since I was real young, maybe 8, so I've been on here 9 years.

tracks.desc: sinking feeling by atelier - this track completely changed my life
meant to be by mavis - made more art listening to this than in a long while
Blue Bird by Lucas Astoria - blue birds flew past us when he first showed me it
your side w/ civility by sim - absolute banger by my favourite AT duo
Lime by Amp7070 - highly underrated and an amazing voice
alibi(improv) - my own track, this is who I want to be musically

intro: 1trillionMPH has made a unique mark on Audiotool, mixing raw, unfiltered noise with striking beauty. Recently won first place in the Audiotool Day 24 Producer Contest.
about: My name is rings aka 1trillionMPH. I'm from Oklahoma!

tracks.desc: carrion by evilpanda107 - the loop remixes moment was so amazing
dojo futon by laevent - Formulation is up there in greatest AT projects
angelic affair by sky bound zoo - choppy percussion like I haven't heard anywhere else
Pills and Syrup by embr - classic, timeless AT sound
SUPERTASK by leadenshrew & evilpanda - gonna go down as one of the best on AT
FARTHER - my own, I realized what music is supposed to be

intro: ryzenn refuses to stick to just one sound. He's always evolving, mixing things up, finding fresh ways to stand out in any genre.
about: Hello! My name Daniel! I go by ryzenn, or taiko! I am from Virginia, 19, currently studying Cybersecurity.

tracks.desc: One of my favorite recent tracks
From my Edition Audiotool album

intro: 'What if thrash metal was chiptune?' virux is one of the most prominent representatives of the rock-oriented scene on Audiotool.
about: I've lived in Connecticut, USA for pretty much my entire life. Currently I'm in college studying graphic design.

tracks.desc: VII by Nova & bonka - some of the best boss music on the site
Delta Phi by looks - one of the best sounding songs I've ever heard, 2014
Small rush by Amp7070 - I believe this is an example of a perfect track
Neuron by Project Epsilon - inspired me to switch from chiptune to dubstep
alibi(improv) by anodyne - I probably wouldn't be making music without him
Fear & Forgiveness - my own, born from struggles with social anxiety

intro: Head Trauma Romance is one of the more club-oriented artists in the Audiotool EDM scene. His music is straightforward, danceable, and true to itself.
about: My name is Alex and I'm a TIG Welder/Manufacturer by day, Head Trauma Romance by night. I live in Richmond, Virginia.

tracks.desc: sinister by X_X - first track with coolest sounds I'd ever heard on this site
La La by Tocka & Avalone - game changing in 2018, still trying to get on this level
Kibbey's house entry - could identify his track blindfolded
UDKBT by TEQTONIQ - huge banger, striving to get on their level
you always— by erthboy - craziest sounds I've heard on AT
SELF DESTRUCT - my own, I think this is my best mix

intro: Dj TeK-NjA is a true drum and bass expert. His minimalist approach is all about pure, unfiltered drum and bass.
about: I'm from West Monroe, Louisiana. I've been with Audiotool about 1yr and a half.

tracks.desc: ah007 by Alex Hoffman - big influence, brings back memories of being a teenager
Thunderstorm by -Seismic- - crazy track, love the thunderstorm background
LOW by HTR & Sub4sax - one of the sickest tracks in Audiotool
No Space, No Light by Snad Breugen - mad respect for this producer
My own track from the Edition Audiotool album

intro: Sub4sax is a master of EDM who knows how to light up any party. Known for high-energy house tracks that keep the crowd moving all night.
about: I am from Slovenia. I am 24 years old. I've been on Audiotool since 2017.

tracks.desc: LOW ft. Sub4sax - collab with Head Trauma Romance
Featured track by HTR & Sub4sax
From my Edition Audiotool album

intro: BioL!nk is an incredibly underrated artist whose exceptional talent deserves way more attention. His music spans from raw drum and bass to house and techno.
about: I'm a 22-year-old music producer from a small village in the north of Portugal. I've been on Audiotool for 7 years now.

tracks.desc: M35 by Snio - one of my biggest inspirations, production incredibly clean
VILE by leadenshrew - one of my biggest inspirations on this platform
Metropia by eratekk - my favorite DnB producer on AT
Covenant by Jetdarc & Client - masterclass in sound design and mixing
Chapter 1: Red Skies by SIREN - captures the essence of his unique style
Starbound (Star VIP) - my own, most fun to make

intro: Batoune & The Brewers has earned something close to legendary status on Audiotool in a surprisingly short time with infectious, pop-tinged fusion of funky house and electro.
about: I am an old French (45 years), I live in a very beautiful region, Provence. I have been on Audiotool for about 3 years.

tracks.desc: LEMON SODA FUNK by Tim Derry - fabulous French artist, reference and model
Out of the walls by Lavalamp - has been by my side since day one
Take Me by Le Voile - introduced me to Drum & Bass
snd-300 by Snad Breugen - a genius, first songs I listened to on AT
Voyage by althruist - wonderful world, so unique
Aurora - my own, a declaration of love to my wife of 20 years

intro: Atelier unfailingly creates a distinct atmosphere with every single piece. From hard-hitting indie guitar tracks to heartbreakingly raw experimental synth soundscapes.
about: Hey, guys! My name is Jack, I'm 19 years old from a small town just outside Fort Worth, Texas. I'm currently serving in the U.S. Army as a paratrooper.

tracks.desc: they love me by :_archive - haunting simplicity and emotional depth
Chop by evilpanda107 - balances raw aggression with clarity
yōsei by etterath - intricate blend of ambient textures
counterpart by nico - clean and intentional, consistently pretty soundscape
Boots! by laevent - noisy, unpredictable, full of personality
sinking feeling - my own track, favourite of my releases

intro: Volen is a hidden gem on Audiotool, quietly making waves with a unique blend of experimental sounds and bass-driven music. Winner of the People's Choice Featured Artist voting.
about: I'm an experimental bass music producer and jazz instrumentalist from McMinnville, Oregon.

tracks.desc: Intricacies by kuatari - so much attention to detail, always mesmerized
grot by Snio - one of the first songs I listened to on AT
Prison Planet by sumad - dark and guttural, biggest influence on percussive sound
Tonal contrast by Roy - one of the best beats I've ever heard
eulogy by sky bound zoo - amazing synth work, biggest recent influence
Daredevil - my own, favorite project to have worked on

intro: noah is undoubtedly one of the top players among the younger generation of Audiotoolers. His music is an electrifying blend of contemporary trap variations.

tracks.desc: lovely. by KC & wvst - one of first wave tracks I heard, inspired my career
Uzeh v. Wutho - had this playing before my mile swim at state championships, I won
.wav by Raiden - super impressive use of synths and lush chords
f1ow by elias & noah - one of the most beautiful masterpieces I've ever heard
LXST NXGHT by Young kay - big inspiration for the fundamentals of my career
i know (w/kc x slow) - my own, one of my greatest works

intro: @elow took third place in our People's Choice Featured Artist voting. His sound spans the full range of contemporary popular music — often noisy, sometimes melodic, always distinctive.
about: What's good My names Elijah Faust. I'm from Slidell Louisiana right above New Orleans.

tracks.desc: Battle of Gods III - my first time listening to the trap side of audiotool
Without You by Zack Reyes - had on repeat at age 14
Warframe Menu Theme - my own track

intro: Chonoes doesn't take prisoners. Fearless, experimental, and unapologetically original.
about: I'm from Greensboro, North Carolina, and I have been on Audiotool since August 2013.

tracks.desc: mothsong by etterath - the most important song to me on Audiotool
see you again by polyspace - brings back nostalgia for childhood trips
the red moon's egg by we die - production is immaculate
Chiliocosm by etterath - best production on Audiotool, hands down
w/ loste & skye by dove & polyspace - hits you like a wall of emotion
REDROOM - my own track

intro: Snowfire crafts experimental ambient soundscapes that spark unsettling, vivid visions in your mind. Over his eight years on Audiotool, he has built a catalog that feels like a forgotten archive of strange transmissions.
about: Hello, I'm a 22 year old Juggalo from upstate South Carolina.

tracks.desc: Empty Promises by no vyce - something about the bouncy anxiety-filled beat
confused by naswalt - listened constantly on the bus in high school
Fartstep by Vulkron - being in discord with him while this was produced
Solitude by Wightfall - inspired me to look at how he did his sound design
Lime by Amp7070 - always stuck out to me most
Untitled(prick/prod) - my own track, best I've published

intro: 14 years ago Alckhem released his very first track on Audiotool. Today he delivers DJ-set-ready, deep techno and house tunes that skillfully balance depth and dance-floor energy.
about: I live in Atlanta, Georgia, USA. I mostly listen to house, dark techno, synthwave, and nu jazz.

tracks.desc: Unbreakable by Kepz
O by oedipax
conjure the ocean by tornsage
Giants by Olaf - great ambient soundscapes
s.st.02 by Sandburgen - unique percussion
ensemble - my own track

intro: evamoth's body of work refuses to be categorized. Escapiist is deeply woven into our community — the embodiment of the contemporary Audiotool artist.
about: I am 17 years old, born and raised in Kansas City, Missouri.

tracks.desc: nåde by elias - loud, distorted, unique
Precinct 9 by Giles - first DnB that made me want to delve in
honest ugly & broken by naswalt - insane
my 816 by Yeetmanz - such a simple yet effective beat
oxygen^2 by po9t - one of the best beats I've heard, period
aliens are real - my own track, got me back into music

intro: erthboy's approach is bass, because that's his element. Still known to many of the OGs under his former name ANK, he has been part of the Audiotool community for an impressive 15 years.
about: I'm Eric. Just a guy haha. I'm from St. George Utah: a half hour from Zion National Park.

tracks.desc: HTR and Sub4sax dropped this track right at a time when I was fully in my UKG era.
This track reminded me of what cool things can be accomplished with Audiotool.
This may as well have been a track by Burial himself.
Alex Hoffman is a HUGE inspiration to me.
THE WORST - this track needs no recognition but it sure deserves it.
Runner's High is what I wish all of my tracks could be.
